subject: Sociology
title: The Civil Rights Movement
description: One-minute skill: The Civil Rights Movement.

hook.text: Four students sat at a whites-only lunch counter in 1960 and refused to leave. Sit-ins spread to 50 cities.

buildup.text: The civil rights movement used nonviolent resistance: boycotts, marches, and legal challenges to dismantle Jim Crow.

discovery.text: It wasn't just marches. Legal strategy, voter registration, and economic boycotts formed a multi-front campaign.

twist.text: The movement is often reduced to King and Parks. Thousands of unnamed organizers did the daily, dangerous work.

climax.text: Civil rights legislation passed. But leaders warned that legal equality without economic justice is hollow.

punchline.text: The movement won laws. The fight for justice didn't end there.

quiz.question: What strategies did the civil rights movement use?
options: Boycotts, legal challenges, and voter registration
